failure_surface: Multiple syntax errors due to natural language/text mixed into code blocks; potential mismatch between transposed data layout and expected output format (test_case_results: 0)
root_cause: The agent generated code containing non-Python text (natural language commentary and XML-style tags) directly in executable cells, causing repeated SyntaxErrors before eventually producing working code. Additionally, the transposition logic did not account for target range boundaries (C1:K6 = 9 columns × 6 rows), potentially writing beyond the required output area.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: Ensure all executable code blocks contain only valid Python statements without interleaved commentary, and always validate that loop iteration counts respect the specified answer_position range boundaries before writing.
evidence: SyntaxError messages on lines 1 showing '<QUESTION_SOLUTION_GENERATION>' tag in code cell
Another SyntaxError showing natural language text 'Now I have enough information...' in code cell
Final successful run used min_row=2 while earlier attempts used min_row=1 (inconsistent row filtering)
Target range C1:K6 spans columns 3-11 (9 columns) but loop iterated col_offset range(20)
Test case returned 0 indicating output did not match expected transposition layout

memory_items.title: Executable Code Purity
memory_items.lesson: Code generation cells must contain only valid Python code; never interleave natural language commentary or markup tags within executable blocks.
memory_items.generalization: When generating spreadsheet manipulation code, isolate all reasoning/thought content from actual executable statements. Use comments (#) sparingly and ensure no XML-style tags or prose appear where the Python interpreter will parse them.

memory_items.title: Range Boundary Validation
memory_items.lesson: Always calculate the maximum cells allowed by the target range before writing data in loops to avoid overwriting unintended areas.
memory_items.generalization: Before executing row/column iteration loops, compute the bounds from the answer position specification (e.g., C1:K6 = rows 1-6, columns 3-11). Use these bounds to constrain inner loops rather than arbitrary large numbers.

memory_items.title: Consistent Data Extraction
memory_items.lesson: Maintain consistent row-starting logic when extracting source data; inconsistent use of header row detection causes unpredictable output.
memory_items.generalization: When extracting values from a worksheet column, explicitly define whether to skip the header row based on context clues (first row contains labels vs. data). Document and apply this decision consistently across all extraction loops.

edits.content:
### 7. Code Block Integrity
- **Pure Executable Content:** Only valid Python code should appear in executable cells. Remove any natural language, thought traces, or markup tags before running.
- **Comment Isolation:** Use inline (#) comments sparingly. Never place block explanations where code expects Python syntax.
- **Tag Separation:** If using special markers (e.g., <SOLUTION>, </SOLUTION>), ensure they exist outside the executable code block entirely.
